title: Button Component
content:
The `Button` component is a flexible and customizable button element for the DocMed UI library. It leverages `class-variance-authority` for defining variants and sizes, and `tailwind-merge` (via `cn`) for efficient class name merging. ## Key Exports - **`Button`**: The main React component for rendering buttons. - **`buttonVariants`**: A `cva` function that generates CSS class strings based on `variant` and `size` props. ## Props The `Button` component accepts standard HTML button attributes, along with the following props: - **`variant`** (`'default' | 'destructive' | 'outline' | 'secondary' | 'ghost' | 'link'`): Defines the visual style of the button. Defaults to `'default'`. - **`size`** (`'default' | 'sm' | 'lg' | 'icon'`): Defines the size of the button. Defaults to `'default'`. - **`asChild`** (`boolean`): If `true`, renders the button using the `Slot` component from `@radix-ui/react-slot`, allowing the button to be a composition of another component. ## Variants - **`default`**: Standard primary button style. - **`destructive`**: For actions that have destructive consequences. - **`outline`**: A bordered button with a transparent background. - **`secondary`**: A secondary button style. - **`ghost`**: A button with no background or border, appearing on hover. - **`link`**: A simple text link style. ## Sizes - **`default`**: Standard button padding and height. - **`sm`**: Smaller button with reduced padding and font size. - **`lg`**: Larger button with increased padding and height. - **`icon`**: A square button typically used for icon-only buttons. ## Usage Example ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; function MyComponent() { return (
); } ```
